About

Shaping the future of decision-making through data and AI

I'm a fresh Information Technology graduate from Princess Nourah bint Abdulrahman University, finishing with Second Class Honors and a 4.59/5.0 GPA. Data Analytics is my primary focus — I like taking a messy dataset through cleaning, exploratory analysis, and KPI design until it becomes a dashboard someone can make decisions from.

At KACST, that meant cleaning and structuring satellite telemetry, writing SQL to extract trends, and building Power BI dashboards used for real operational reporting. I bring the same habits — cleaning, EDA, visualization, clear reporting — into my AI work too, from preparing datasets for a COPD-diagnosis model to structuring reports for an LLM-based classification pipeline.

I recently completed an AI Engineering Bootcamp with the Saudi Digital Academy to go deeper on the AI side, and I'm now looking for my first full-time role as a Data Analyst — with Machine Learning / AI Engineer roles also a strong fit.

Selected Work

Featured Projects

Four projects spanning space-tech, healthcare, and public-sector AI.

01 · KACST — Space Technologies

Saudi Satellites Interactive Dashboard

Built during my KACST internship: a web dashboard visualizing open-source historical data on Saudi satellites, enabling exploration of orbital trends and operational statistics.

Power BI SQL Data Visualization

02 · AI Engineering Bootcamp

COPD Vision

An AI-driven X-ray classification model built with deep learning and RAG, designed to diagnose and grade COPD severity — integrated with a clinical chatbot that provides real-time decision support.

Deep Learning RAG Clinical Chatbot

03 · SDA Hackathon

Tabyun

An LLM-powered system built for Nazaha that classifies administrative corruption reports and prioritizes high-risk cases for a faster governmental response.

LLMs NLP Classification

04 · Graduation Project

Wifaq

Wifaq (Arabic for "harmony") is an AI-powered mobile platform that forms complementary university project teams. Instead of students self-selecting friend groups, Wifaq matches them by skill profile so every team ends up with a balanced mix of leadership, design, and development strengths.

< /p>

Problem

Self-picked project teams tend to skew toward friend groups, often leaving teams unbalanced — several designers and no developer, for example.

Solution

Students fill out a skill profile; a matching algorithm groups them so each team has a complementary mix of roles before work even starts.

My role

Designed the matching logic, prepared and analyzed the student skill-profile data, and worked on the Flutter front end.

Technologies

Python · Flutter · Matching algorithm

AI / Matching logic

Each student's profile (role preference, skills, past project ratings) is compared against classmates to form groups that balance leadership, design, and development coverage rather than optimizing for friendship or convenience.

Results

Delivered as a working mobile app with team formation, role tagging, task tracking, file sharing, and in-app discussion — presented as my graduation project.

Machine Learning Exploratory Data Analysis Python Flutter

05 · Analytics Case Studies

Power BI Dashboards

A selection of dashboards built for population, retail, and telemetry analysis.

Saudi population analysis — gender split, growth 2010–2022, nationality mix.

Retail sales performance — channel, customer type, and regional breakdown.

COVID-19 global analysis — total cases, deaths, recovery rate, and regional case density.

06 · Excel Analytics

Excel Dashboards

Interactive Excel dashboards built with PivotTables, slicers, and KPI cards to turn raw datasets into a decision-ready view.

Saudi Population Analysis Dashboard

Business problem: Tracking how Saudi Arabia's population has grown and shifted by gender, nationality, and region between 2010–2022.

Data used: Yearly population records by gender, nationality (Saudi/non-Saudi), and region, 2010–2022.

KPIs: Population by gender split, Saudi vs. non-Saudi ratio, and total population growth trend by year.

Insights & recommendations: Year and nationality slicers make it possible to isolate growth trends for Riyadh specifically, useful for regional planning discussions.

Retail Sales Performance Dashboard

Business problem: Sales managers needed a single view of performance across channels, cities, and customer segments to guide monthly decisions.

Data used: Invoice-level sales data with manager, employee, product category, city, customer type, and date fields.

KPIs: Sales by channel, city, customer type and gender, monthly sales trend, and top products by revenue.

Insights & recommendations: Online sales dominate the channel mix and loyal customers drive the largest share of revenue — pointing to loyalty programs and online channel investment as priorities.
